meta.name: dy-data UI Design System Candidate
meta.purpose: Provide the complete V0.2 candidate design system for human review before runtime migration.

principles.name: 运营后台优先
principles.description: 界面服务高频筛选、比对、跟进和审核，不做营销页式装饰。

principles.name: 轻量 token 先行
principles.description: 先固化颜色、字号、间距、半径、控件高度、层级和关键组件规则，再逐步迁移页面。

principles.name: 可视化决策
principles.description: 重要规范值必须能在 HTML 预览里被人类直接看到，而不是只存在于文字清单。

principles.name: 浅色模式优先
principles.description: V0.1 只固化浅色模式；暗色模式不跟随系统设置，也不作为当前设计系统承诺。

principles.name: 规范需要校验
principles.description: 关键 token 与已实现 CSS 通过测试绑定，后续再扩展到 lint、视觉回归和 PR checklist。

principles.name: 品牌色与语义色分离
principles.description: 候选品牌橙只承担品牌、主操作、选中和聚焦；成功、警告、错误和信息继续使用各自语义色。

green.role: Primary action, active state, success tone

brandDeepOrange.role: Primary action background with white text

brandOrange.role: Brand accent, focus and selected indicator

family.value: "Microsoft YaHei", "PingFang SC", "Noto Sans CJK SC", system-ui, sans-serif

caption.usage: Metadata and compact code labels only

sm.usage: Small tags and compact inner elements

xl.usage: Mobile sheets and large modal corners

heightMd.usage: Desktop button, select and input height

library.usage: 唯一 UI 图标渲染库

family.usage: 唯一 UI 图标图形来源

componentEntry.usage: 业务代码通过统一组件引用 Solar 图标

tableActionSize.usage: 表格内查看、复制等小动作图标

styleRule.value: Solar linear by default; bold-duotone only for active mobile navigation or brand emphasis.
styleRule.usage: 避免同一界面混用多种图标风格

variantSelection.namingRule: Register semantic names in SolarIcon.tsx; split normal and emphasis states explicitly, such as cluesLine and clues.

semanticExamples.logout: logout linear unless used as a highlighted module action
semanticExamples.clues: chat round dots bold-duotone only when active

previewContract.rendering: One hidden SVG symbol per registered semantic icon; every visible sample references it with <use>.

pageMaxWidth.usage: Public entry and standalone pages can use a max width; authenticated workspace pages do not center on this value.

workspacePageWidth.usage: All authenticated workspace pages fill the available shell width. Individual content sections, tables or dialogs own their internal reading width.

dataWorkspaceWidth.usage: Desktop detail data workspaces inherit the full workspace width and add fixed viewport height plus internal result scrolling

dataWorkspaceHeight.usage: Desktop detail data workspaces stay within one viewport below the global topbar

tableStickyGap.usage: Gap between frozen table header and sticky navigation

tabletBreakpoint.usage: Tablet and mobile shell starts at max-width 920px

tableHeaderCorner.usage: Sticky header plus sticky first column corner

shadowNone.usage: Table cells, chips and inline controls keep hierarchy through borders instead of elevation.

shadowCard.usage: Cards, filter panels and regular content surfaces.

shadowPopover.usage: Select menus, comboboxes and contextual floating panels.

shadowDialog.usage: Dialog and ConfirmDialog surfaces above page content.

shadowWorkbench.usage: Large operational workbenches such as clue follow-up.

button.rule: Buttons and form controls share the same base radius and height.
button.usage: Use primary for page or card main action; use danger only for destructive confirmation actions.

field.selectIndicator: CSS chevron, no native browser arrow and no text glyph.
field.dropdownSurface: Select and combobox expanded states share the same menu surface, option highlight and shadow.
field.expandedPreview: Use custom button or combobox triggers for Select previews in this document to avoid the browser native dropdown overlapping the designed menu.
field.rule: Inputs, selects and textareas use one border and focus logic.

iconButton.rule: Icon-only controls must have an accessible name and use SolarIcon.

families.StatusChip: Business state such as pending, success, warning and danger.
families.CountPill: Result count, selected count or aggregate quantity.
families.FilterChip: Active filter summary with a clear affordance.

chip.rule: Do not reuse StatusChip for counts, filters, roles or code tokens.

filterBar.mobileBehavior: Collapsed by default, with a visible collapse action at the bottom when open.

metricCard.standard: Neutral monitoring value with no top accent.
metricCard.primary: One page-level key value with a deep orange top accent.
metricCard.semantic: Operational state value using existing semantic green, blue, amber or danger accents.
metricCard.loading: Fixed-size skeleton that preserves the final card geometry.
metricCard.rule: Use metric cards only for at-a-glance monitoring values, not repeated page sections. One view has at most one primary card.

navigation.desktopRail: Primary modules use Solar linear icons by default; the active module uses bold-duotone and a soft orange background.
navigation.desktopSubnav: Secondary navigation stays compact with a 2px deep orange active underline.
navigation.mobileTopSubnav: Current module sub-navigation remains horizontally scrollable at the top on small screens.
navigation.rule: The four-item mobile sample is an information architecture candidate only; it does not create runtime routes.

tertiary.mobileOverflow: horizontal scroll with current item visible

dialog.confirmUsage: Use for destructive, permission-changing, bulk or irreversible actions.

clueFollowUpWorkbench.mobileBehavior: Near-full-screen single-column workbench with natural internal order: status and phone actions, follow-up action, order/product/order-time summary, then round history.
contentRegions: sticky header with title and close action
first left row for contact, phone reveal/copy actions and lead status
second left row for product, order id, order time, order status and verification status
third left row for grouped round history
right action rail for follow-up form and guidance
grouped round history where each round owns its follow-up records
clueFollowUpWorkbench.rule: Use this larger modal only for operational clue follow-up. It is not the default Dialog size and should not expose internal assignment ids in store-facing copy.

clueFollowUpMobileDetail.scrollArea: single-column natural task flow with local content scroll in preview only
clueFollowUpMobileDetail.touchTarget: 44px minimum for reveal, copy and save controls
contentOrder: order, product and order time summary
round history and nested follow-up records
clueFollowUpMobileDetail.rule: Mobile clue detail is a dedicated task page, not a squeezed desktop modal. It must not show data-origin labels or internal assignment ids in store-facing copy.

dataTable.rule: Desktop tables can freeze headers below the top action bar; mobile switches to record cards when fields cannot fit.
dataTable.stickyRule: Sticky header stays inside the table scroll container; page-level navigation offset is handled by the container, not each th.
dataTable.workspaceRule: On desktop detail data workspaces, the page frame does not scroll; the table container owns vertical scrolling and pagination remains visible.
dataTable.paginationRule: Pagination belongs to the result section footer outside the table scroll container. Desktop shows range, page size, first/previous, editable page number with a jump action, next/last. Mobile keeps previous, editable page number and next; Enter jumps without an extra jump button.
dataTable.paginationValidation: Page number input accepts positive integers from 1 to totalPages and shows an adjacent error such as 请输入 1-447 之间的页码.
dataTable.loadingRule: Skeleton rows should match the final table row structure.

mobileClueCard.rule: Mobile clue cards show the same operational decision fields without requiring horizontal scrolling.

desktopShell.rule: The rail and topbar should not repeat the page H1; pages own the only H1.

mobileShell.rule: Mobile uses bottom first-level navigation and top second-level navigation.

appliesTo: stable subpages inside one secondary workspace
tertiaryNavigation.desktopPlacement: after page heading and before filters or content
tertiaryNavigation.mobilePlacement: after page heading and before content
tertiaryNavigation.routingRule: Every item has a stable URL and supports refresh, deep links, and browser history.
tertiaryNavigation.stickyRule: Do not add an independent sticky layer or change table sticky offsets.

listPage.rule: List pages should keep the same scan order across settlement, clues, orders and admin lists.

detailDataWorkspace.desktopRule: Authenticated workspace pages use a full-width page-frame by default. Desktop detail pages that are primarily dense tables additionally use a fixed viewport workspace: page-frame width 100%, height calc(100vh - topbar), no outer page scroll, and table region scrolls inside the result section.
detailDataWorkspace.mobileRule: Mobile does not use the fixed-height workspace; it returns to natural page scroll and card/list presentation.

mobileDetailWorkspace.filterBehavior: collapsible filter panel with active filter chips and an explicit collapse action
mobileDetailWorkspace.scrollRule: Use natural page scrolling; never compress the desktop wide table into the mobile viewport.

desktopStructure: header with title and close action
left main column with contact and phone actions, lead status, product/order/order-time detail and round-grouped history
right action rail with follow-up form and guidance
mobileStructure: order, product and order time summary
round history and nested follow-up records
clueFollowUpWorkbenchModal.desktopRule: The clue follow-up modal is a task workbench, not a small detail dialog: width min(1440px, calc(100vw - 48px)), height min(920px, calc(100dvh - 32px)), internal body scroll only.
clueFollowUpWorkbenchModal.permissionRule: Store-facing copy keeps invalid rounds visible for rate context, but full phone reveal/copy and follow-up editing are allowed only for the current active round.

structure: phone, reveal/copy actions, lead status and current round
follow-up result selector, note field and full-width save action
order id, order time, order status, product and verification status
clueFollowUpMobileDetail.rule: Mobile implementation should be a one-column task flow with phone/status and follow-up operation before audit history.
clueFollowUpMobileDetail.permissionRule: Invalid, verified or refunded clues keep masked phone visible but disable full phone reveal/copy and follow-up editing.

current: Keep key app runtime CSS tokens aligned with this file through apps/web/src/design-tokens.css and tests/test_design_system_docs.py.
Keep the design-system adoption contract documented in docs/design-system/README.md.
Keep current static enforcement covered by tests/test_design_system_enforcement.py.
Review docs/design-system/index.html when deciding a new token value.
Do not introduce new one-off colors, radii or control heights without updating tokens.json.
Keep the design-system preview light-only until dark mode is intentionally designed and tested.
Do not add ad hoc UI icons; extend SolarIcon.tsx and this icon token set instead.
Keep desktop detail data workspaces on the page-frame--data-workspace template when the primary task is filtering and reviewing dense records.
Do not import @iconify/react or @iconify-icons/solar directly outside SolarIcon.tsx.
Block unauthorized hex, rgb, hsl and oklch colors outside apps/web/src/design-tokens.css.
Block business pages from using legacy status-chip, filter-chip, feedback-status-chip and metric-card class entries.
Block div or span click handlers that should be native buttons or links.
next: Add visual regression screenshots for core components.
Add a PR checklist for new pages and components.
Generate apps/web/src/design-tokens.css from tokens.json instead of syncing it by hand.

deepOrange.role: Primary action background with white text
deepOrange.usage: Critical submit actions, next-step actions and primary pagination

orange.usage: Brand marks, selected indicators, focus rings and high-attention accents

softOrange.usage: Selected navigation, filter chips, current page and low-intensity notices

black.usage: Headings, body text and text placed on the base orange

softGray.usage: Table headers, recessed regions and alternating neutral backgrounds

white.usage: Panels, cards, tables, inputs and dialogs

primaryButton.loadingRule: Preserve width and prevent duplicate submission

brandMark.rule: Use the enlarged trend and status-node mark centered in a soft orange rounded square.

approvalGate.rule: Candidate tokens remain documentation-only until explicit human approval is recorded in Linear.
